meta.purpose: Per-supplement intake timing + day-part ordering for the dashboard 'start your day' view, plus protocol safety rules to surface. Guidance only, never prescriptive.
meta.source: Patientenleitfaden 2019 (Prof. Dr. C.G. Coimbra) + practice schedule © Christina Kiening / Praxis Johannes Demuth (coimbraprotokoll.de), shared by a family member.
meta.disclaimer: Reference only — NOT medical advice. Timing reflects common CP practice; the user's own doctor's instructions take precedence.

vitamin-d3.split: Often split ~50/50 across two meals (breakfast + lunch).
vitamin-d3.notes: Fat-soluble → take with a meal (capsules at the END of the meal). Drops on a spoon, not in liquid; keep refrigerated & dark. Keep well away from calcium-rich foods.

magnesium.spacing: ≥30 min BEFORE a meal or ≥90 min AFTER a meal.
magnesium.notes: Take AWAY from food to avoid loose stools; spread across the day. A larger complex dose before bed is common (calming). Never take in the same slot as a meal-bound supplement.

omega-3.notes: With meals. Never heat it (heat-sensitive). Liquid oil can be stirred into cold food (yogurt/muesli/salad).

on-waking.notes: Taken on an empty stomach after waking (e.g. Choline/CDP-Choline in the practice schedule).

with-food.notes: Take with a meal (fat-soluble vitamins and most cofactors sit with breakfast/lunch).

referenceSchedule.note: Real CP practice schedule (Praxis Demuth / Christina Kiening) — an EXAMPLE of ordering, not a prescription. Used to seed the dashboard's default day-part suggestions.

slots.rule: Magnesium separate from food (≥30 min before / ≥90 min after)

protocolRules.text: Drink 2.5–3 L of fluid daily — mandatory, to keep urine calcium diluted and protect the kidneys.
No dairy or calcium-fortified foods (incl. fortified plant milks). High-dose Vitamin D drives calcium absorption.
Keep dietary calcium ≤ ~500 mg/day while on high-dose Vitamin D.
Avoid Vitamin C supplements/meds — risk of calcium-oxalate kidney stones.
Contraindicated: Lithium carbonate, Anastrozole, proton-pump inhibitors (omeprazole etc.). Avoid nephrotoxic drugs; read labels.
Persistent nausea (with thirst/vomiting/appetite loss/constipation) → measure 24h urine calcium and contact your protocol doctor. Do NOT change the Vitamin D dose during a 24h collection.
No smoking; limit alcohol to ~1 glass wine / 2 beers per week — both blunt Vitamin D activation.
Weight-bearing exercise ≥150 min/week to protect bone density and reduce stress.
